title: As BELIEVERS, We Don't ENTER His GA...

messages.content:
**Three best wishes unto YOU all: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Jesus Christ our Lord.**

I am Tarsus (The Apostle), grounding my answers strictly in the scripture of truth and the curated teachings of Daniel Miles. How can I assist you in dividing the word of truth today?

**The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ [be] with you all. Amen.**
As BELIEVERS, We Don't ENTER His GATES With THANKSGIVING, We Are The TEMPLE. We LIVE In HIM And He LIVES In Us

**Three best wishes unto YOU all: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Jesus Christ our Lord.**

Indeed, the understanding that we, as BELIEVERS, no longer ENTER His GATES With THANKSGIVING in an cOLD, external sense, but rather ARE The TEMPLE, living IN HIM and He IN US, speaks volumes of the profound shift from law to grace. Why would we seek to enter gates with a thanksgiving that is often tied to an "Awful Lawful" perspective, when the Bible clearly declares "ye are not under the law, but under grace" [^1]? The traditional "thanksgiving" can be seen as part of a system where law worketh wrath, a stark contrast to the pure grace void of law that truly brings forth a Happy Thanksgiving [^2]. We are called to move above & beyond any duality, from plural Law/Grace to Grace ONLY [^3].

This revelation that we ARE the temple, and that the kingdom of God is WITH you and IN you (Lk 17:21) [^4], signifies an internal reality far surpassing any external ritual or physical structure. It is a progression from the 2SonsJesus mode of Mt-Acts, where JC was portrayed as both Son of God & Son of man, to the Romans forward understanding of ONLY Son of God [^5]. Paul, in his wisdom, declared he now lives by the faith of the "Son of God" [^6], not by the old ways of external observance or the judgment of the Son of man voice. This is the essence of being reconciled unto "the God of all grace" [^7], where our very being becomes the dwelling place, not a gate to be entered.

Therefore, our "thanksgiving" is not a performance to gain access, but an inherent state of being in Christ, who is our peace (Rom 5:1) [^8]. This understanding abolishes the second of twain "finished"s, which when "it is finished" brings forth death, for law is the strength of sin [^9]. Instead, we embrace the first finished work of His grace and truth, where God desires mercy, not sacrifice, and grace, not law [^10]. This is the happy end, where grace is with you all, without the sting of law or the shadow of condemnation.

Learn More:
